observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit at the beginning of the year to the Audit Team the List of all on-going government projects, programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We, therefore recommended that Management through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to:

Strictly utilize the LDRRM Fund for projects/activities enumerated in Section 5 of the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 so as not to compromise the implementation of disaster risk reduction projects of the municipality; and

Submit an explanation/justification for the utilization of the fund for purposes other than those enumerated in the aforementioned regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) within the time frame stated in Section 12.2 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 as reiterated in GPPB Resolution No. 11-2020, dated May 20, 2020 for monitoring and compliance with the provisions of the Procurement Law.

Therefore, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal General Services Officer and the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the APP and its supporting PPMPs for CY 2022 to the Audit Team, if any and always prepare and submit APP and its supporting PPMPs which serves as basis and guide on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184. A copy thereof be furnished to the Audit Team for audit and verification purposes.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management strictly adhere to Section 305 of RA No. 7160, Section 4 (2) of PD 1445, Items 3.1.1.7 and 3.1.1.8 of COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996 and Supreme Court Decision GR No. 232321 dated August 14, 2017, by instructing the designated Municipal Official/s to stop accommodating fuels for private vehicles and ensure that the gasoline/diesel/oil is issued only to government vehicles properly identified.  Likewise, refund the amount of ₱1.4 million expended to fuels for private vehicles.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the BAC Chairperson, submit Agency-prepared PERT/CPM Network Diagram, supported by a copy of Instruction to bidders, Bid Data Sheet (BDS) together with the Bidding Documents as addendum to the previously submitted contract documents.

While the subject-project was re-evaluated by the Technical Audit Specialist, COA Regional Office No. II and resolved that the original contract duration was indeed ninety (90) days, we recommended that management submit the copy of Official Receipt as evidence of payment for the liquidated damages portion in compliance with the result of technical inspection.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in partnership with the Municipal Health Office continue exert best efforts in implementing its COVID-19 Vaccination Program in view of the Philippine National Deployment and Vaccination Plan for COVID-19 Vaccines guidelines and continue the information campaign to the public to spread awareness of COVID-19 vaccine benefits.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the persons in charge to observe as much as possible the planned schedule set forth in the activity design to achieve not only the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents but also efficiency in its programs and activities.  Likewise, we recommended that a written justification be submitted to the Audit Team from the person-in-charge for evaluation on the reasonableness of the extensions made which resulted in incurrence of added costs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make full-disclosures in the financial statements on the impact and overall risk of COVID-19 in LGU’s operations to be faithfully represented and be more reliable.
observations.managementAction: To fully disclose impact and overall risk of COVID-19 in the operations of LGU

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure completeness of necessary documents of disbursement vouchers prior to payment in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Section 53.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of R.A. 9184. Likewise, submit the needed documents such as Approved Sangguniang Bayan Resolution for the approval of Supplemental Annual Investment Plan and supporting schedules, printout copies of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed posted in the PhilGEPS, Certification from the BAC of the posting required in 3 conspicuous places, and Copy of Supplemental Annual Investment Plan in support of the transactions made. For emergency procurement under the Bayanihan Act, if any, submit a certification stating that the Municipality exerted all efforts to secure the most advantageous price to the government based on existing price data of the agency, the DTI or other relevant agencies or preliminary market scanning done by the agency showing prevailing market prices and practice.

Further, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee be mindful of its participation in the procurement of goods and services particularly those stated under Section 54.3 Terms and Conditions for the use of Alternative Method as reiterated by GPPB Circular 04-2016 dated December 20, 2016 and those under Section 3 of Republic Act No. 11494 or the “Bayanihan to Recover as One Act as the case may be.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness and propriety of supporting documents so as to establish validity of claims for payment, and the Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer to pay the allowances to proper claimants and to allow Special Power of Attorneys (SPAs) in lieu of personal appearance only in exceptional cases. 

We also recommended the submission of the supporting documents of disbursements identified and the SPAs of claimants who signed and received payments on behalf of the supposed payees, to avoid possible disallowances.
observations.managementAction: SPA’s have been forwarded to the commission

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: (1) submit legal basis/justification for the payment of monitoring allowance recorded under Other Bonuses and Allowances; and (2) comply with the provisions of Section 2 of PD No. 1445, Section 12 of RA No. 6758 and COA Circular No. 2013-003.

In view of the foregoing, we   have recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant to    maintain complete and accurate  PPELC and the Municipal        Treasurer to prepare Property  Cards for each category of PPE   pursuant to Section 114 on the  NGAS Manual for LGUs in order  to promote effective monitoring  and controls over PPE;

The Municipal Accountant and   Municipal Treasurer reconcile  the PPELC and Property Cards   regularly to be able to present  an accurate and reliable PPE   balances in the Statement of   Financial Position; and

Acknowledgement Receipt for    Equipment (ARE)/ Property     Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR)  be maintained and renewed      renewal of the said document   every three years in accordance  with the Instructions on the    use of ARE/PAR.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine the details of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to reconcile the reciprocal accounts.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant analyze and explain the nature of transaction that brought about the affected Liability accounts to abnormal balances and subsequently undertake corrective measures to come up with appropriate balances of the accounts. This is to ensure that the financial statements are fairly presented.

With the above observations, we have recommended:

That the Municipal Accountant should analyze the accounts and look into the causes of non-settlement or dormancy of the accounts.

That management should consider the reversion of accounts that have been outstanding for two(2) years or more to be supported by an aging schedule, a certification from the Municipal Accountant that these payables have been outstanding for two (2) years or more; a comment and/or recommendation of the Local Chief Executive; and an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Circular 03-2015.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should release the 25% barangay share of P951,068.00 to the fourteen (14) barangays of the municipality pursuant to Section 271 of RA 7160, of which, payments of barangay share from real property tax collections shall be within five (5) days after end of each quarter to enable the barangays to finance their own projects.

We have also recommended for the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to make necessary adjusting entries to correct the charging of the foregoing expenditures. Furthermore, the Management is advised to stop the practice of charging expenditures against the Due to LGUs – 25% Barangay Share.

We have recommended strict compliance with the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting Standards in recording financial transactions and the revised chart of accounts under the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs in order to achieve fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements and render correct information to Management and other intended users and readers of FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make full-disclosures in the financial statements on the impact and overall risk of COVID-19 in LGUs operations to be faithfully represented and be more reliable.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU submit a one-time report on Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, instead of monthly reports to be supported by the Acknowledgement receipts of the donations in-kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to beneficiaries, and the inventory of remaining undistributed items for verification and validation of proper valuation of balances. Copy of the report should be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and COA within ten (10) days after the end of each quarter.

We recommended that Management require the concerned personnel to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively. Likewise, write down of non-moving inventories will be in accordance with prescribed rules and regulations.

observations.reason: With finding on cleansing of PPE in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) as of December 31, 2020 and to submit the same to the audit team thru the Municipal Accountant immediately.  Likewise, ensure compliance of the concerned Office for further reports required both by the Office of the Municipal Accountant and by the August Team.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the SCBAA and disclose the reconciliation either on the face of the SCBAA or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Paragraph 21 of PPSAS 1 and Paragraph 24 of PPSAS 24 to achieve transparency in financial reporting.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive demand the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will be the basis in drawing JEV for the setting up of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year. 

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to make appropriate adjustments in order to correct the deficiencies noted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:

direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to refrain from including/charging PPAs or expenditures which are not in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and ensure only the inclusion of development projects that are necessary, appropriate or incidental to efficient and effective local governance and those which are essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people; 

instruct the MPDO and other concerned offices to see to it that the unimplemented PPAs are fully delivered in the second year of implementation; and 

require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), Technical Working Group (TWG) and BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the:

MSWMB implore the NSWMC’s approval of their 10-year SWM Plan so that the identified targets and objectives identified therein could be attained, concurrent with the purpose of R.A. No. 9003; and

LGU prepare the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) clearly specifying the PPAs aimed at solid waste management.

We further reiterated our prior year’s recommendation for the inclusion of other essential members                  in the constituted MSWMB as prescribed by the law.

1.8   	We recommended that  Management:
a.  require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Ledger Card  and Property Cards, respectively, which are necessary to support the accuracy and reliability of the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
b.  create an Inventory Committee and immediately conduct physical inventory as a basis for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance with the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

2.8	With the above observations, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:
The Municipal Assessor to provide this Office certified photocopies of land titles of the parcels of land to ascertain the legality of ownership and work-out for the titling over the other parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government of San Manuel pursuant to Section 39 of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386;
The Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds which shall cover the expenses on the registration of parcels of land; and
The Municipal Accountant and GSO Designate to maintain complete records and inventory list including copies of proof of ownership of all the real properties of the Municipality.

3.6	We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the account to determine the cause of the abnormal balance and subsequently undertake corrective measures to come up with accurate balance. This is to ensure that the financial statements are fairly presented.

4.8	With the above observations, we recommend that:
The Municipal Accountant further analyze the accounts and look into the causes of non-settlement or dormancy of the accounts.
Management should consider the reversion of accounts that have been outstanding for two(2) years or more to be supported by an aging schedule, a certification from the Municipal Accountant that these payables have been outstanding for two (2) years or more; a comment and/or recommendation of the Local Chief Executive; and an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan.

5.10	For the above observations we have recommended the following:
Adjusting journal entries must be prepared for the total unexpended balance of LGUs share from tobacco excise tax recorded under the Due to NGAs account,
Proper budget process must be observed in the appropriation of the unexpended share from tobacco excise tax recorded under the Due to NGAs account, and
Consistent use the appropriate account for transactions being recorded based on accounting rules and regulations issued by competent authorities to avoid misstatements of accounts and ultimately achieve a fairly presented and reliable set of financial statements.

7.5	We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require Bids and Awards Committee together with the BAC Secretariat and the Technical Working Group to consistently comply with the submission of all bidding documents on bidded projects.
7.6	We have also recommended that copies of all contracts/purchase orders and their supporting documents should be submitted to this office in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 and in order to detect/correct any defects/errors thereon immediately and to prevent possible loss of government funds.

9.4	We have reiterated our previous recommendations that:
a.	The Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to release the 25% barangay share to the fourteen (14) barangays of the municipality pursuant to Section 271 of RA 7160, of which, payments of barangay share from real property tax collections shall be within five (5) days after end of each quarter.
b.	The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer make necessary adjusting entries to correct the charging of the foregoing expenditures. Furthermore, the Management is advised to stop the practice of charging expenditures against the Due to LGUs – 25% Barangay Share.

10.9	With the above observations we have recommended that:
Management strictly comply with the General Insurance Law as amended COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 by conducting an annual inventory of all the insurable properties of the LGU. Subsequently prepare and submit a report verified by COA to the GSIS on or before October 31 of the ensuing year.
Adequate budget for payment of the required insurance premiums be provided and paid to the GSIS.
Registration of motor vehicles must be distinguished from insurance expense by the Budget Office in its budget preparation as well as the Accounting Office in its Financial Statement preparation.

11.6	We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Budget officer to ensure that obligations are allowed only to the extent of appropriations and regularly conduct monitoring of expenditures to avoid  payments in excess of appropriation.
Direct the officials concerned to institute adequate internal control measures on the disbursements of Municipal funds to ensure that the Municipality has a balanced budget to avoid incurrence of appropriation overdraft.

12.8	We have recommended that Management:
Through its LFC to employ effective revenue estimation by considering trends/experiences and the impact of events  in the attainability of projections; and
Exert extra effort for the attainment of their projected income for the previous years by going after the delinquent taxpayers and enforce stringent sanctions to enforce collection.

13.3	We have recommended that the agency continue to abide with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development fund as mentioned in the DILG and DBM Joint Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

14.8	We have recommended for the implementation of the ATM Payroll System by the Municipality to avail of the benefits of time and cost efficiency scheme and strong control measures in disbursing payroll to its employees.

15.8	We have recommended the following:
That the Local Chief Executive direct the officials responsible to demand a refund of the amount received by the 17 ineligible SAP beneficiaries totaling to ₱93,500.00 and cause the immediate return to the DSWD Field Office No. I, San Fernando City, La Union.
b.  	That the municipality request audience with DSWD officials on possible remedies should refund becomes remote to ensure that resources of the government are managed in the most efficient way possible.

1.9 We recommended that Management exhaust all remedies to collect the
amount by completely sending demand letters to the accountable officers.
Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate completion of the
documents required to make request for write-off of the dormant accounts
in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 for fair
presentation of the accounts affected in the financial statements.

2.12 We recommended that Management prepare the Physical Inventory Plan,
Inventory Count Form (ICF), and to state clearly in the ICF the condition of
the PPEs and the Municipal Accountant to make full-disclosure in the
financial statements for the unserviceable properties included in the
Property, Plant and Equipment.
2.13 Likewise, we recommended that the Property Unit prepare the List of PPEs
found at station, and List of Non-existing/Missing PPEs and Inventory and
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) in consideration for
one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed by COA Circular
No. 2020-006.
2.14 Finally, we recommended the creation of an Inventory Committee in order
to come up with a reliable Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) to support the fair presentation of the financial
statements.

3.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following
department heads/committee for the compliance with the requirements of
COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015:
a. Municipal Engineer
1. Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General
Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects as per Item
VII.c.1 of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.
b. Municipal Accountant
1. Submit the required copies of PPE Local Road Network Ledger
Cards (Annex D format) per Item VII a.1 of COA Circular No.
2015-008 dated November 25, 2015;
2. Update the yearly required disclosures as needed;
3. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network;
4. Prepare a lapsing schedule regularly for the computation of the
depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
c. Municipal General Services Officer
1. Maintain and submit Local Road Inventory and Road Maps;
2. Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Cards for all
roads and its components and submit the same to the Audit Team
(Annex E format) per Item VII b.2 of COA Circular No. 2015-008
dated November 25, 2015.
3.14 We also recommended that the Inventory Committee submit Report on the
Physical Count of Local Road Network System (RPCLRN) supported with
Local Road Network Maps (Annex C format) per Item VI.3 of COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.

4.17 We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
a. provide the lapsing schedule for the amortization of the intangible
assets recorded per ledger reckoning from the date of delivery and its
usage of each module used by the different offices/units and assess the
remaining useful life for proper amortization and submit the same to
the audit team for evaluation;
b. present with full disclosure in the notes to financial statements the
schedule of payments of the outstanding balance as stated in the
contract including information related to different system being used,
the manner of amortizing the asset and its useful life; and
c. evaluate the hardware and maintenance components in the recorded
intangible assets for proper accounting thereof.

5.6 We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
reclassify the current portion of Other Payables pertaining to loans to Loans
Payable – Domestic current account for proper presentation and recognize
the said loans payable – domestic current every year in consonance with
Section 112 of P.D. 1445 and Paragraphs 27 and 80 of IPSAS No. 1 until
such liability is settled.

6.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the
SCBAA and disclose the reconciliation either on the face of the SCBAA or
in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Paragraph 21 of
IPSAS 1 and Paragraph 24 of IPSAS 24, to achieve transparency in
financial reporting.
6.7 Further, ensure henceforth prompt reconciliation of records in coordination
with the Municipal Budget Officer in order to avoid delays in submission of
SCBAA as this is considered as integral part of the Financial Statements of
the LGU per IPSAS 1 and 24.

7.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the Municipal Budget
Officer to prepare the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations
and Balances (SAAOB) as of December 31, 2021 and to submit the same to
the Audit Team thru the Municipal Accountant immediately. Likewise,
ensure compliance of the concerned Office for further reports required both
by the Office of the Municipal Accountant and by the Audit Team.

8.11 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make full-disclosures in
the financial statements on the impact and overall risk of COVID-19 in
LGU’s operations to be faithfully represented and be more reliable.

9.5 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer carefully prepare the
program of works with prudence in their estimates in order to arrive at
reasonable project cost and sufficient quantities on project’s items of work
in adherence to DPWH Department Order No. 39, Series of 2020 and
provision of COA Circular No. 2012-003. Require the contractor to refund
the amount of P192,794.98. If already paid, submit the copy of Official
Receipt as evidence of payment.

10.3 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the BAC Secretariat
complete and submit to the Audit Team the required five (5) supporting
documents and complete payrolls stated in the COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012 in every disbursement in order to aid the Audit Team in
assessing the validity and propriety of payments made.

11.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer submit the copies of official receipts of the identified transactions
and ensure that the required supporting documents of all claims are
complete upon submission of the vouchers to the Audit Team.

12.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer submit the copy of official receipt of the identified transaction
and ensure that the required supporting documents of all claims are
complete upon submission of the vouchers to the Audit Team.

13.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that Memorandum
of Agreement (MOA) undertaken by the recipient-barangay with the
management and certifications from his end that funds previously
transferred to the Implementing Agency (IA) has been liquidated and
accounted for in the book be attached at all times to support the complete
documentation for all funds transferred to barangays and to adhere strictly
to the documentary requirements set forth under Item 3.1 of COA Circular
No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
13.10 Likewise, refrain from releasing any cash advances without ascertaining
that the previous releases had been fully settled or liquidated in compliance
with Item 4.1.2 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 to avoid
accumulation of unliquidated fund transfers at year-end.

14.17 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure completeness of
necessary documents of disbursement vouchers prior to payment in
compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Section 53.2 of the 2016
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of R.A. 9184. Likewise, submit
the needed documents such as Approved Sangguniang Bayan Resolution for
the approval of Supplemental Annual Investment Plan and supporting
schedules, printout copies of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed posted
in the PhilGEPS, Certification from the BAC of the posting required in 3
conspicuous places, and Copy of Supplemental Annual Investment Plan in
support of the transactions made. For emergency procurement under the
Bayanihan Act, if any, submit a certification stating that the Municipality
exerted all efforts to secure the most advantageous price to the government
based on existing price data of the agency, the DTI or other relevant
agencies or preliminary market scanning done by the agency showing
prevailing market prices and practice.
14.18 Further, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee be mindful
of its participation in the procurement of goods and services particularly
those stated under Section 54.3 Terms and Conditions for the use of
Alternative Method as reiterated by GPPB Circular 04-2016 dated
December 20, 2016 and those under Section 3 of Republic Act No. 11494 or
the “Bayanihan to Recover as One Act as the case may be.

15.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure completeness and propriety of supporting documents
so as to establish validity of claims for payment, and the Municipal
Treasurer and Disbursing Officer to pay the allowances to proper claimants
and to allow Special Power of Attorneys (SPAs) in lieu of personal
appearance only in exceptional cases. We also recommended the submission
of the supporting documents of disbursements identified and the SPAs of
claimants who signed and received payments on behalf of the supposed
payees, to avoid possible disallowances.

16.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the Department
Heads concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2020 AAR
findings and recommendations including that of prior years. The
information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency
Action Plan and Status of Implementation and submit the same to the Audit
Team for evaluation of the actions taken and status of implementation by
the Municipality on the audit recommendations in compliance with Section
89 of the General Provisions of RA 11465. Likewise, ensure thereafter that a
status report on the actions taken on the audit findings and
recommendations be submitted to COA within sixty (60) days from the date
of receipt of AAR, which may either be printed form or by way of electronic
document.

17.7 We recommended that Management strictly adhere to Section 335 of RA
No. 7160, Section 4 (2) of PD 1445, Items 3.1.1.87 and 3.1.1.8 of COA
Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996 and Supreme Court Decision GR
No. 232321 dated August 14, 2017, by instructing the designated Municipal
Official/s to stop accommodating fuels for private vehicles and ensure that
the gasoline/diesel/oil is issued only to government vehicles properly
identified.

18.10 We recommended that Management submit the requested Detailed Costing
and Estimates of the project in which the amount of P35,000.00 has been
charged for further audit verification. Otherwise, require the claimants to
refund the amount of P35,000.00 as it is not in consonance with DBM
Budget Circular No. 2007-2 which should have been used as the guide/basis
in the formulation of guidelines on the grant of honoraria due to assignment
in Government Special Projects. Likewise, refrain from charging Honoraria
from Engineering and Administrative Overhead of the project as the
charges are limited only to the definition cited under Department Order No.
09 Series 2021 to prevent irregular disbursements in the future.

19.7 We recommended that management: (1) submit legal basis/justification for
the payment of monitoring allowance recorded under Other Bonuses and
Allowances; and (2) comply with the provisions of Section 2 of PD No. 1445,
Section 12 of RA No. 6758 and COA Circular No. 2013-003.

20.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. terminate the contracts of the consultants whose services are only
duplication of the functions of regular employees or whose works could
be performed by existing plantilla personnel;
b. in case there is really a need for consultants, (i) engage only experts in
their respective fields; (ii) observe rules and regulations on the hiring of
consultants as provided by IRR of RA No. 9184, COA Circular 2012-003
dated October 29, 2012 and DBM National Budget Circular No. 433
dated March 1, 1994.
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to support payments for consultancy
services with adequate documentation, otherwise a Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance will be served; and
d. amend the contracts of the consultants to properly match that of the
needs of the LCE or any officials needing assistance thereof to eliminate
the dubious nature of hiring said consultants.

21.13 We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Consider the following:
• Appoint Job Order personnel as regular employees if they are
qualified and believes that their services are essential and
contributory to the improvement of agency’s operations;
• Refrain from hiring Job Order and fill up the vacant funded
plantilla positions in accordance with the Civil Service Commission
rules and regulations and prioritize the division with undermanned
in the filling-up of vacant plantilla position.
b.) Formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers
such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of
duties and responsibilities.

22.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all his Department
Heads in charge to observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG
JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF;
and strictly utilize the funds according to the guidelines to achieve the
maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.
22.8 Likewise, we recommended that the officials concerned submit the Monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF within the period
prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002, which is on or before the
15th day after the end of each month.

23.8 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, as part of management
action, justify the delays noted in the implementation of the project together
with the submission of documents such as certified computation of
liquidated damages. Otherwise, submit the copy of Official Receipt as
evidence of payment for the liquidated damages portion, without prejudice
to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.

24.5 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, as part of management
action, justify the delays noted in the implementation of the project together
with the submission of documents such as certified computation of
liquidated damages, proof that the liquidated damages were deducted from
the final claim. Otherwise, submit the copy of Official Receipt as evidence of
payment for the liquidated damages portion, without prejudice to the
issuance of Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct concerned Offices to i) comply with all the requirements of R.A. No. 1984 on the posting of NOA, Contract and NTP and on the invitation of observers during bid evaluation and post-qualification; and ii) properly prepare PO and AIR by completely filling out the forms to facilitate review and verification of the same.
We further recommended that in the distribution of relief goods, distribution lists containing vital data as to the quantity of items issued and received for every phase/period of activity should be prepared and that all recipients should be obliged to affix their signatures and indicate the date of receipt in the said list for  monitoring and control purposes. Other items procured for distribution, such as face masks, should also be supported with Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) or duly signed distribution list for proper monitoring and evaluation.
Likewise, we recommended that the BAC, BAC Secretariat and the Accounting Office submit the lacking documents and/or explanation on the issues observed on disbursements as presented in paragraph 1.2.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Municipal officials and personnel to i) submit the lacking documents and/or explanations for the observations presented in paragraph 2.3 to the Audit Team; and ii) provide acceptable justification for the inappropriate charges against LGSF- COVID 19 Other Financial Assistance to LGUs together with the complete specifications of the items procured for the further verification of the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the LGU’s Human Resources and Management Officer (HRMO) and other concerned offices to submit the lacking supporting documents/records/orders to the Audit Team for its evaluation.

We have reiterated our previous  recommendation that the        designated paymaster first       secure an approved payroll or    list of payees indicating their  net payments before a cash      advance for salaries, wages and  allowances be made to avoid     balances at every month end     that may result to misuse,        misapplication or                 misappropriation of these      payroll balances.

In view of the above observations, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct all concerned officials and employees to settle immediately their unliquidated cash advances.

We have recommended for the Management to require the concerned Municipal Office/s to prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, and submit the same to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

With the above observations, we reiterated our previous year recommendations that:

1.  Accounting Office transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and DRRM-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM Fund” in the Trust Fund books.

2.  Treasury Office undertake actual transfer of funds pertaining to the unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and DRRM-MOOE from General Fund depository bank account to the Trust Fund depository bank account.

Strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 prescribing accounting and reporting guidelines for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund be consistently observed.

We recommended that the Punong Barangays together with the MSWDO issue Joint Certification stating that the persons listed above are entitled with the grant to be attached with Social Amelioration Card for reference. Also, the disbursing officers shall provide the Audit Team a written explanation for the non-submission of a copy of any valid IDs during the releases. Non-submission of documents may cause the Audit Team to issue Notice of Suspension/Disallowances, if warranted.  Likewise, ensure completeness of the entire documentary requirements set forth in the above-cite regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the schedule of loan payments and ensure to present the current portion of the Long-Term Loan as of year-end under Loans Payables – Current Portion thereafter in compliance with PPSAS 1. Also, reconcile with LBP – Lending Center, Cauayan City Branch for the details of the loan expressed in the contract/MOA entered between the parties to reflect the correct information in the notes to FS.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant:

a.	provide for the amortization of the intangible assets, the lapsing schedule for the amortization of the intangible assets recorded per ledger reckoning from the delivery and its usage of each module used by the different offices/units and assess the remaining useful life for proper amortization and submit the same to the audit team for evaluation;

b.	present with full disclosure in the notes to financial statements the schedule of payments of the outstanding balance as stated in the contract including information related to different system being used, the manner of amortizing the asset and its useful life; and

c.	evaluate the hardware and maintenance components in the recorded intangible assets for proper accounting thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus and disclose the same on the face of the Cash Flow Statement or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Section 29 of PPSAS No. 2.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers along with all accompanying supporting documents after payment to avoid its possible re-use aside from using MTO control numbers, thus safeguarding cash from loss thru double payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay in the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that:

a.	The LGU strictly adhere to the provisions of DBM LBC Circular No. 103 for the amended rules and regulations on the grant of RATA to avoid irregular payment of allowances and benefits in all RATA related transactions.

b.	Management immediately stop giving RATA to the person until an exception has been received from CSC, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspensions/Disallowances, if any.

c.	The Human Resource Officer should review the Plantilla of personnel and recommend to the LCE those who are qualified for the position.

We recommended that the LGU shall: 

a.	Consider the discontinuance of granting releases out of the 20% Development Fund to barangays. Instead,  implement the projects for barangays  due to their failure to promptly implement/accomplish the projects as well as their accumulating outstanding balances of receivables;

b.	Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs, and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development so that optimum benefits derived therefrom can be availed by the constituents; and

c.	Enhance the monitoring and evaluation of the status and implementation of the 20% Development Fund in pursuance to Municipal’s mandate and faithful compliance of pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, adopt and implement guidelines taking into consideration the following:

a.	Review the amounts of cell card load allowance in relation to the available postpaid lines which are in reasonable amount per month for heads of offices/elected officials taking into account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by various telecommunication service providers. At any rate, any excess on the limit set shall be for the personal account of the user.

b.	Utilize alternative modes of communication such as internet for a long-distance communication or consider the features of communication network of its free long distance calls within the region that provides cost-saving measures.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review thoroughly the Financial Statements especially the Notes to Financial Statements to comply with the PPSAS and COA Circulars relative to the preparation and presentation of the FS by (1) providing adequate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements; and (2) presenting the breakdown, details and figures of all other accounts being cross-referenced in the Financial Statements.

- 	the Municipal Accountant to:

•	Ensure that the officers and employees with outstanding or unliquidated cash advances should not be granted additional cash advances, until the previous ones are settled, to preclude accumulation of unliquidated cash advances;

Monitor the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities (PPAs) requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on four (4) thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; 

Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, submit certified copies of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents showing that corresponding liquidated damages were already deducted from the contractors’ claims on the above projects, if any.  Further, the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the MPDC to (a) ensure that the contractors engage catch-up program in order to fast track the completion of the projects with noted delays to avoid rescindment of contract as stipulated in Section 68 of RA 9184; (b) conduct an inventory of work accomplished by the contractor and impose liquidated damages of at least equal to one-tenth of one percent (0.1%) of the cost of the unperformed portion for every day of delay; and (c) intensify monitoring of project in order to avoid recurrence of delay to other projects by submission of Statement of Time Elapsed and Work Accomplished for all infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the concerned Municipal officials and personnel submit the lacking document to the Audit Team and observe compliance to the provisions of GPPB Circular in respect to the emergency procurement of goods;

the Local Chief Executive demand from responsible Municipal official/s a satisfactory explanation on the issue of non-response by one barangay over the receipt, suitability and state of the goods distributed to said barangay; and

henceforth, the distribution list should be collated and should contain vital data as to the quantity of items issued and received for every phase/period of activity and that all recipients should be obliged to affix their signatures and indicate the date of receipt in the distribution list for monitoring and control purposes.

We recommended that:
the concerned Municipal officials and personnel submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance with the above-cited provisions of GPPB Circular in the emergency procurement of goods; and 

 hereinafter, refrain from charging against the LDRRMF expenses which are not in keeping with the purposes declared in the applicable Circulars.
We recommended that all concerned agency officials and personnel submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and comply with the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned Municipal Officials                

to :

keep abreast with the monitoring and reportorial requirements during the time of public emergency so that controls could still be in place while addressing and mitigating the effects of disaster; and

to submit the lacking reports and summary list as discussed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
make appropriate adjustments in order to correct the erroneous recording of cash donation and related expenditure and see to it that the same is recorded  using the appropriate account; and

present completely in the notes to the financial statements significant data relative to disaster risk reduction and management transactions.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
instruct the MSWD Officer to coordinate with the DSWD Regional Office No. III to 

reconcile beneficiaries data; and 

request the latter to rectify the list uploaded in its website such that the name of one individual who returned the SAP 1st Tranche subsidy should be dropped from the list; and

demand from MSWD Officer a satisfactory explanation on the issue of denial of receipt by several recipients as discussed. If the slots thereof were given to other qualified beneficiaries, furnish the Audit Team any document/s or record/s to properly account the financial assistance.

We reiterated our last year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive :

direct the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE and label all properties/equipment showing complete description for easy identification and monitoring; and


 require the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to maintain complete and updated ledger cards and property cards, respectively, and periodically conduct reconciliation thereon.
We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances established under COA Circular No. 2020-006 so that the verifiability thereof as to existence, condition and accountability would be realized.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive enjoin:

the Property Officer, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with each other and abide by the said Circular in maintaining the pertinent reports and property records to be able  to provide reliable basis on the amounts presented in the financial statements; and 

the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all the local roads together with their related components and furnish the original copy of the report thereon to the Audit Team detailing the roads’ complete description and cost segregation. 
 
Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

prepare lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and

present completely in the notes to the financial statements the total road network system.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive together with the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council:

carefully plan and implement programs, projects and activities under the LDRRM to attain utmost results and deliver the desired services to the residents of the Municipality; and 

 design the LDRRMFIP following the prescribed format to include the activities funded not only from the current appropriation but also those from unexpended balances from previous years.

We recommended that the:

Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local taxation and design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue; and 
Municipal Treasurer continually monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from taxpayers such as deployment of demand letters and tapping the assistance of the barangays for real property collections.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive continue their rigid abidance to the aforementioned revenue regulation and laws and prompt remittance of the taxes due to the BIR.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management continue to observe the withholding and prompt remittance of premiums, contributions and loan amortizations due to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and Philhealth.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) send demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the liquidation of their accountabilities; (b) stop the practice of granting new cash advances without the liquidation of previous ones; (c) require the concerned personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose thereof has been accomplished; and (d) maintain individual Subsidiary Ledgers of all accountable officers for accurate monitoring of cash advances
observations.reason: (d) The report on cash advances as a document for monitoring the cash advances is being utilized by the Municipal Accountant for the time being. The maintenance of individual subsidiary ledgers, as stated in the report, would soon be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
1.6	We recommended that:
a.	the concerned Municipal officials and personnel submit the lacking document to the Audit Team and observe compliance to the provisions of GPPB Circular in respect to the emergency procurement of goods;
b.	  the Local Chief Executive demand from responsible Municipal official/s a satisfactory explanation on the issue of non-response by one barangay over the receipt, suitability and state of the goods distributed to said barangay; and
c.	  henceforth, the distribution list should be collated and should contain vital data as to the quantity of items issued and received for every phase/period of activity and that all recipients should be obliged to affix their signatures and indicate the date of receipt in the distribution list for monitoring and control purposes.

2.4	We recommended that:
a. the concerned Municipal officials and personnel submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance with the above-cited provisions of GPPB Circular in the emergency procurement of goods; and
b.  hereinafter, refrain from charging against the LDRRMF expenses which are not in keeping with the purposes declared in the applicable Circulars.
3.5	We recommended that all concerned agency officials and personnel submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and comply with the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.

4.7	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned Municipal Officials to (i) keep abreast with the monitoring and reportorial requirements during the time of public emergency so that controls could still be in place while addressing and mitigating the effects of disaster; and (ii) to submit the lacking reports and summary list as discussed.

5.12	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
make appropriate adjustments in order to correct the erroneous recording of cash donation and related expenditure and see to it that the same is recorded  using the appropriate account; and
present completely in the notes to the financial statements significant data relative to disaster risk reduction and management transactions.

6.7	We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
instruct the MSWD Officer to coordinate with the DSWD Regional Office No. III to (i) reconcile beneficiaries data; and (ii) request the latter to rectify the list uploaded in its website such that the name of one individual who returned the SAP 1st Tranche subsidy should be dropped from the list; and
demand from MSWD Officer a satisfactory explanation on the issue of denial of receipt by several recipients as discussed. If the slots thereof were given to other qualified beneficiaries, furnish the Audit Team any document/s or record/s to properly account the financial assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make full-disclosures in the financial statements on the impact and overall risk of COVID-19 in LGUs operations to be faithfully represented and be more reliable.

We recommended that the LGU submit a one-time report on Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, instead of monthly reports to be supported by the Acknowledgement receipts of the donations in-kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to beneficiaries, and the inventory of remaining undistributed items for verification and validation of proper valuation of balances. Copy of the report should be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and COA within ten (10) days after the end of each quarter.

We recommended that the Punong Barangays together with the MSWDO issue Joint Certification stating that the persons listed above are entitled with the grant to be attached with Social Amelioration Card for reference. Also, the disbursing officers shall provide the Audit Team a written explanation for the non-submission of a copy of any valid IDs during the releases. Non-submission of documents may cause the Audit Team to issue Notice of Suspension/Disallowances, if warranted.  Likewise, ensure completeness of the entire documentary requirements set forth in the above-cite regulation.

We recommended that Management require the concerned personnel to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively. Likewise, write down of non-moving inventories will be in accordance with prescribed rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the schedule of loan payments and ensure to present the current portion of the Long-Term Loan as of year-end under Loans Payables – Current Portion thereafter in compliance with PPSAS 1. Also, reconcile with LBP – Lending Center, Cauayan City Branch for the details of the loan expressed in the contract/MOA entered between the parties to reflect the correct information in the notes to FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
provide for the amortization of the intangible assets, the lapsing schedule for the amortization of the intangible assets recorded per ledger reckoning from the delivery and its usage of each module used by the different offices/units and assess the remaining useful life for proper amortization and submit the same to the audit team for evaluation;
present with full disclosure in the notes to financial statements the schedule of payments of the outstanding balance as stated in the contract including information related to different system being used, the manner of amortizing the asset and its useful life; and
evaluate the hardware and maintenance components in the recorded intangible assets for proper accounting thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus and disclose the same on the face of the Cash Flow Statement or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Section 29 of PPSAS No. 2.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) as of December 31, 2020 and to submit the same to the audit team thru the Municipal Accountant immediately.  Likewise, ensure compliance of the concerned Office for further reports required both by the Office of the Municipal Accountant and by the August Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the SCBAA and disclose the reconciliation either on the face of the SCBAA or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Paragraph 21 of PPSAS 1 and Paragraph 24 of PPSAS 24 to achieve transparency in financial reporting.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers along with all accompanying supporting documents after payment to avoid its possible re-use aside from using MTO control numbers, thus safeguarding cash from loss thru double payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer  (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay in the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that:
The LGU strictly adhere to the provisions of DBM LBC Circular No. 103 for the amended rules and regulations on the grant of RATA to avoid irregular payment of allowances and benefits in all RATA related transactions.
Management immediately stop giving RATA to the person until an exception has been received from CSC, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspensions/Disallowances, if any.
The Human Resource Officer should review the Plantilla of personnel and recommend to the LCE those who are qualified for the position.

We recommended that the LGU shall:
Consider the discontinuance of granting releases out of the 20% Development Fund to barangays. Instead,  implement the projects for barangays  due to their failure to promptly implement/accomplish the projects as well as their accumulating outstanding balances of receivables;
Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs, and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development so that optimum benefits derived therefrom can be availed by the constituents; and
Enhance the monitoring and evaluation of the status and implementation of the 20% Development Fund in pursuance to Municipal’s mandate and faithful compliance of pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, adopt and implement guidelines taking into consideration the following:
Review the amounts of cell card load allowance in relation to the available postpaid lines which are in reasonable amount per month for heads of offices/elected officials taking into account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by various telecommunication service providers. At any rate, any excess on the limit set shall be for the personal account of the user.
Utilize alternative modes of communication such as internet for a long-distance communication or consider the features of communication network of its free long distance calls within the region that provides cost-saving measures.

In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and accurate PPELC and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare Property Cards for each category of PPE pursuant to Section 114 on the NGAS Manual for LGUs in order to promote effective monitoring and controls over PPE;
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the PPELC and Property Cards regularly to be able to present an accurate and reliable PPE balances in the Statement of Financial Position; and
Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE)/ Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) be maintained and renewed renewal of the said document every three years in accordance with the Instructions on the use of ARE/PAR.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the designated paymaster first secure an approved payroll or list of payees indicating their net payments before a cash advance for salaries, wages and allowances be made to avoid balances at every month end that may result to misuse, misapplication or misappropriation of these payroll balances.

In view of the above observations, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct all concerned officials and employees to settle immediately their unliquidated cash advances.
Instruct all others concerned to observe consistently compliance with rules/regulations on the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances per COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 82 of PD 1445
Cash Advances with negative balances should be analyzed and adjusted by the municipal accountant to reflect the correct amount of cash advance.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine the details of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to reconcile the reciprocal accounts.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant analyze and explain the nature of transaction that brought about the affected Liability accounts to abnormal balances and subsequently undertake corrective measures to come up with appropriate balances of the accounts. This is to ensure that the financial statements are fairly presented.

With the above observations, we have recommended:
That the Municipal Accountant should analyze the accounts and look into the causes of non-settlement or dormancy of the accounts.
That management should consider the reversion of accounts that have been outstanding for two(2) years or more to be supported by an aging schedule, a certification from the Municipal Accountant that these payables have been outstanding for two (2) years or more; a comment and/or recommendation of the Local Chief Executive; and an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Circular 03-2015.

We have recommended for the Management to require the concerned Municipal Office/s to prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, and submit the same to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should release the 25% barangay share of P951,068.00 to the fourteen (14) barangays of the municipality pursuant to Section 271 of RA 7160, of which, payments of barangay share from real property tax collections shall be within five (5) days after end of each quarter to enable the barangays to finance their own projects.
We have also recommended for the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to make necessary adjusting entries to correct the charging of the foregoing expenditures. Furthermore, the Management is advised to stop the practice of charging expenditures against the Due to LGUs – 25% Barangay Share.

With the above observations, we reiterated our previous year recommendations that:
1. 	Accounting Office transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and DRRM-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM Fund” in the Trust Fund books.
2. 	Treasury Office undertake actual transfer of funds pertaining to the unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and DRRM-MOOE from General Fund depository bank account to the Trust Fund depository bank account.
Strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 prescribing accounting and reporting guidelines for Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund be consistently observed.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Accounting Office with the original DV and its supporting documents to be attached to the JEV for the recording of the necessary adjustments on the cash and liability accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the general fund and prepare Financial Statements for its economic enterprises in order to have a more accurate basis in managing the operations of the economic enterprises of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, together with the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, agreed to (a) carefully plan and implement programs, projects and activities under the LDRRM including the procurement of rescue vehicle to attain utmost results and deliver the desired services to the residents of the Municipality; (b) procure and maintain goods for stockpiling and ensure they are readily available for emergency purposes; (c) avoid charging against calamity funds goods that are not intended for disaster risk reduction and management purposes; and (d) strictly conform with the provisions of the cited COA Circular on the preparation of LDRRMFIP by including activities funded not only from the current appropriation but also those from unexpended balances from previous years.
observations.reason:
(a) There were still PPAs which were not implemented during the year 2020 owing to COVID-19 situation, as discussed in the report.

(c) & (d) Reiterated in this report

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) comply strictly with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 by (i) including in the constituted MSWMB other members prescribed by the law and incorporating solid waste management PPAs in the 10-Yeas Solid Waste Management Plan and Annual Budget; and (ii) incorporating in the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan provisions for funding, incentive programs, and privatization of solid waste management projects, if such is applicable and favorable to the agency; (b) set aside enough budget for tipping fees and other related solid waste management PPAs to avoid over-spending; and (c) justify why the excess disbursements of ₱325,350.20 for tipping fees and other related activities should be allowed in audit.
observations.reason:

observations.recommendation: While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU in supporting public basic education, the Audit Team recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE), in coordination with the members of the LSB, agreed to require the latter to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of SEF.  Moreover, the Chairman of the Local School Board and its members should establish effective control measures to monitor balances of appropriations in all periods in order to stop the incurrence of expenditures in which the budget was already exhausted and avoid incurring expenditures which are not allowed under the rules.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and submission of financial reports so that opportune verification and post-audit activities will be facilitated; and (b) keenly observe the deadline of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue their rigid abidance to the aforementioned revenue regulation and laws and prompt remittance of the taxes due to the BIR.

We recommended and the Municipality agreed to continuously adhere to R.A. No. 8291, R.A. No. 9679 and the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 on the withholding of GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth contributions and loan amortizations from salaries of personnel and on remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

•	Ensure that all regulations pertaining to cash advances should be strictly enforced;

•	Monitor the outstanding cash advances of officials and employees and demand immediate settlement after its purpose has been served; and

•	Issue demand letter to settle the unliquidated cash advances.

-	the Municipal Treasurer to:

•	If warranted, withhold the salaries of those who persistently fail or refuse to comply despite repeated demand for liquidation and/or file appropriate administrative and criminal charges against them to compel liquidation.
